title: Jap ji Sahib Stik
gloss: Shalokas from the Jap ji with translation and commentary.
notes: Japji Sahib (the core Sikh morning prayer) with commentary, in Persian script from the Naqshbandi Mujaddidi press. Date printed '1909' (likely 1910).

flags.issue: year printed 1909; likely 1910 given quarter context

author: Kahla Singh
title: Jhagra Man Dhi
gloss: Verses in the form of a calendar, containing a dialogue between a mother and her daughter on love, in which the former tries in vain to dissuade the latter from indulging in guilty love.

author: Kali Das
title: Gopi Chand
gloss: This story is noticed at entry No. 152 on page 11 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1909.
notes: Raja Gopichand renunciation legend, 7th edition. Dual date: Sammat 1966. Same Kali Das as p12 s18 (Rup Basant).

author: Kali Das
title: Puran Bhagat
gloss: This story is noticed at entry No. 49 on page 13 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1909.
notes: Reg 2 (second-registered book of the year). Puran Bhagat legend, 4th edition. Dual date: Sammat 1966. Bracket note 'Previous edition noticed' (cut off).

author: Karam Chand
title: Bhajan Mihra Sadhar
gloss: Hymns in praise of God and admonitory verses.

author: Lahora Singh, Bhai
title: Hir ba Taswir
gloss: The well-known story of the love of Hir and Ranjha.
copyright: Bhai Lahora Singh, Photographer, Dabbi Bazar, Lahore; No. 32, dated the 21st March, 1910.
notes: An illustrated Hir ('Hir with pictures') by B. Lahora Singh, photographer of Dabbi Bazar, Lahore — the SAME photographer-publisher who produced the copyrighted Guru Nanak photographs in 1910Q4 (PUNJABI—ART); early instance of photographically-illustrated qissa print.

title: Majmua Qissa Shamshad Khetri wa Larka
gloss: Verses describing a miracle of the Prophet Muhammad and the generosity of Ali, the Fourth Caliph, etc.
notes: THE entry cross-referenced from 1910Q4 (p15 s293, Majmua-i-Qissa-i-Shamshad: 'see entry No. 58 at page 17 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st March 1910') — a forward-reference now resolved within our own extraction set.

title: Majmua Si Harfi
gloss: A collection of love verses by Ilahi Bakhsh, Bibi Yasin and Mian Sharaf Din.
publisher: Fakir Muhammad Sharif, Gakhar Chima, Gujránwála district
notes: Anthology including a woman poet (Bibi Yasin) — female authorship in the si-harfi tradition.

author: Mansha Ram
title: Jhok Allah Wali
gloss: Verses directed against the teaching of the Quran and observance of rituals.
notes: Arya Samaj polemic against Quranic teaching (Hindu author Mansha Ram, Arya Steam Press), free — the Arya–Muslim polemic front already active in Q1 (cf. the polemic ring of Q4).
